metadata.title:
RESERVOIR ON CLIFF PARK MEANS


NO PROTECTION FOR NORTH SIDE

pages.raw_text:
There are some reasonably well In
kane who profess to believe that the
city has made serious and costly mis-
takes in the construction of the pres
ent water system. For example, they
claim that IL better water supply than
is now afforded might have been OBJ
tanned by sinking wells in the vi
entity OF the present pumping sta
ton: that this could have been done
at n comparatively small expense and
that thereby the city would have
been saved the greater part of the
heavy cost of the great ship canal
which now conveys the water from
the dam down to the power house


They say that, In any event, this
canal was unnecessary, except to
catch the logs, brush. chips and gen.
eral notsam and jetsam brought down
by the river and hold it in suspen-
sion until 1t rots or is raked out.
They say, too. that the power for
the pumps could have been supplied
by the Washington Water Power
company at n cost to the city much
less than Is now paid as interest on
the money expended in the construc-
tion OF the dam at that point.


However these things may be, 1t be-
hooves us to see that no mistakes
are made in the location or construc-
ton OF the reservoir which 1t Is now
proposed to build. We think, there
fore, that the committee Of engineers,
to whom the plans presented by City
Engineer gill have been referred by
the city council for examination. will
not only scrutinize them closely, but
will also carefully examine and re-
port upon the proper site to be Sc
lectcd for the erection of the reser-
voir. The cost of the reservoir on
the Cliff Park site. together with the
installation of additional pumps and
mains, is estimated by Mr. Gill to be
about $4,000. The actual cost of
the construction OF these works on
his plans Is more likely to exceed
than to fall below his estimates. but
even If they should be constructed
within his estimates, this will bring
us dangerously near the constitution
al limit of Indebtedness which the
city may incur for light and water
purposes. If 1t be true. as suggested
by Mr.. Pratt at the last meeting of
the city council, when this reservoir
matter was before that body for con-
sideration. that an available site
could be had In the foothills Just
north OF the pumping station. 1t
seems to us that this would be the
most desirable site for it's location.
We are led to this belief because of
the fact that reservoir located at
this point would give protection to
the entire city. and not to only that
portion Of 1t located on the south
